title: Le Morte Darthur
summary: The pinned repository XML and a current public ETS-form XML are two structurally distinct representations of University of Michigan CME item MaloryWks2, a 1997 electronic transcription of the Caxton-derived text in H. Oskar Sommer's 1889 volume 1. Sommer copied the incomplete Althorp/Earl Spencer copy of Caxton's Westminster print of 31 July 1485, now University of Manchester reference 18930, and supplied twenty-one missing printed pages on eleven historical facsimile leaves made from the then-Osterley copy, now Morgan PML 17560; he also had those passages collated against that original. The exact manuscript copy delivered to Caxton is unidentified. British Library Additional MS 59678 is an independent, incomplete parallel witness to the same earlier Malory textual layer, not an upstream source of the CME transcription. Complete local research surrogates preserve all 874 canvases of the Rylands bound print and all 970 PDF pages of the surviving Winchester codex; exact work ranges and image-rights constraints are recorded below.
review_notes: The intellectual work, Caxton's print state, the two surviving physical copies, Sommer's three-volume edition, its scan derivatives, the U-M encoding, and the repository artifact are modeled separately.

Sommer volume 1 is the immediate source edition. Volumes 2 and 3 are companion bibliography, apparatus, collation, glossary, and source studies; they are supporting sources, not sources of the encoded readings.

The two Internet Archive objects for Sommer volume 1 are consecutive scan parts: part 1 covers front matter through printed p. 424 and part 2 covers printed pp. 425-861. Neither file alone is a complete scan of the volume.

Sommer's account identifies the Earl Spencer/Althorp copy as his base and the Osterley/Pope copy as the source used to check twenty-one supplied pages. Their documented provenances identify those objects with present Manchester 18930 and Morgan PML 17560.

British Library Additional MS 59678 and Caxton's print preserve materially different versions. The graph treats the manuscript as a supporting parallel witness to a shared earlier textual layer and never as the source from which Sommer or CME was transcribed.

The complete Rylands IIIF bundle is cached at 1200-pixel width per canvas. The encoded textual object maps to canvases 7-867; covers, modern notes, blank and end leaves, and binding views remain in the complete 874-canvas bundle.

The complete surviving Winchester codex is cached as a 970-page public Internet Archive PDF. Its medieval text maps to PDF pp. 11-956 (folios 9r-484v, with the cataloged losses); covers, endleaves, and edge views remain in the file.

Manchester's images are CC BY-NC-SA 4.0. The Winchester Internet Archive item applies a Public Domain Mark, but every manuscript image bears an embedded British Library copyright notice; the conflict is recorded and local caching is not treated as redistribution permission.

The current U-M item says Public Domain / No Copyright-United States, while the 1997 XML header carries restrictive legacy availability terms. Both dated rights layers are preserved pending explicit provider clarification.

The current public raw XML at medictionary.info is not byte-identical to the pinned DLPSTEXTCLASS file. It wraps the text as ETS/EEBO, converts the title page into a DIV1/P, removes EPB milestones, renames HI1/NOTE1, expands correction attributes into 93 CHOICE pairs, and supplies one additional word (‘ye’) at printed p. 249.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for MaloryWks2
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The file is 1,987,982 bytes and uses the older DLPSTEXTCLASS encoding family.
Its exact transfer event from U-M and any post-1997 byte-level normalization remain undocumented.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item MaloryWks2
entities.description: A keyed and structurally marked-up electronic transcription of Sommer volume 1's Caxton-derived text, excluding Sommer's front matter and editorial notes while supplying some structural headings and recording later corrections.
notes: The XML credits Sencor with keyboarding and says HTI subsequently proofed and corrected the full volume.
The header's language declaration lists only Latin, although the primary text is Middle English and only fifteen FOREIGN elements are explicitly tagged Latin.
The encoding contains Caxton's preface, table, and twenty-one books in 502 chapter DIV2 containers, plus a generated title-page unit.

entities.label: Current public ETS-form XML for MaloryWks2 at medictionary.info
entities.description: A current public raw XML representation of the U-M item in an ETS/EEBO wrapper. It carries a public-domain availability statement and mechanically restructures several legacy elements, but also supplies one word absent from the pinned DLPSTEXTCLASS representation.
notes: The file is 2,016,882 bytes and was valid XML when cached.
It has 24 DIV1 and 502 DIV2 elements, compared with 23 DIV1 and 502 DIV2 in the pinned file; the extra DIV1 is the converted title-page wrapper.

It has 93 CHOICE/CORR/SIC pairs and one SUPPLIED element. A normalized token comparison that retained the first child of each CHOICE found the same text as the pinned representation except for SUPPLIED ‘ye’ in ‘I wende ye wold not’ at printed p. 249.

entities.label: H. Oskar Sommer's three-volume Le Morte Darthur (1889-1891)
entities.description: Sommer's three-volume edition: volume 1 prints the Caxton text; volume 2 supplies bibliographical, textual, linguistic, and lexical apparatus; volume 3 studies Malory's sources and includes Andrew Lang's essay.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Malory, Le Morte Darthur, the Original Edition of William Caxton Now Reprinted and Edited by H. Oskar Sommer, 3 vols. (London: David Nutt, 1889-1891).
bibliographic.title: Le Morte Darthur

entities.label: Sommer, Le Morte Darthur, volume 1: Text (1889)
entities.description: Sommer's modern-type, page-for-page and line-for-line transcription of Caxton's 1485 print, based on the Earl Spencer/Althorp copy and checked against it throughout. Twenty-one pages absent from that copy are printed from Whittaker facsimiles made from the Osterley copy and are marked with asterisks.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Malory, Le Morte Darthur, vol. 1, Text, ed. H. Oskar Sommer (London: David Nutt, 1889).
bibliographic.title: Le Morte Darthur

notes: Sommer's prefatory explanation and volume 2 specify the base-copy and replacement-page workflow.
The twenty-one asterisked pages are 195-196, 303-306, 613-614, 623-624, 713-716, and 855-861.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Sommer volume 1, part 1
entities.description: The first of two consecutive University of Michigan scan objects for Sommer volume 1, containing the title and prefatory matter through printed p. 424.
entities.reproduction_format: 442-page PDF scan plus OCR and scan metadata derivatives
notes: This is a complete derivative of the IA sub-object but partial coverage of Sommer volume 1.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Sommer volume 1, part 2
entities.description: The second of two consecutive University of Michigan scan objects for Sommer volume 1, containing printed pp. 425-861.
entities.reproduction_format: 440-page PDF scan plus OCR and scan metadata derivatives
notes: This is a complete derivative of the IA sub-object but partial coverage of Sommer volume 1.

entities.label: Sommer, Le Morte Darthur, volume 2: Introduction (1890)
entities.description: The companion apparatus volume, including the bibliographical history, relation of editions, statement of editorial method, errors in Caxton, collation of Whittaker facsimile leaves with the Osterley originals, language notes, headings, name and place index, and glossary.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Malory, Le Morte Darthur, vol. 2, Introduction, ed. H. Oskar Sommer (London: David Nutt, 1890).
bibliographic.title: Le Morte Darthur

notes: The Internet Archive metadata gives the set date 1889, but the scanned volume 2 title page and preface explicitly date this volume to 1890.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Sommer volume 2
entities.description: A complete 246-page University of Michigan scan object for Sommer's 1890 apparatus volume.
entities.reproduction_format: 246-page PDF scan plus OCR and scan metadata derivatives

entities.label: Sommer, Le Morte Darthur, volume 3: Studies on the Sources (1891)
entities.description: Sommer's section-by-section source study with an introductory essay on Malory's prose style by Andrew Lang and an appendix printing previously unpublished French source material.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Malory, Le Morte Darthur, vol. 3, Studies on the Sources, ed. H. Oskar Sommer, with an introductory essay by Andrew Lang (London: David Nutt, 1891).
bibliographic.title: Le Morte Darthur

notes: This historical source study is useful evidence but is not treated as a current exhaustive account of Malory's source relationships.
The Internet Archive metadata gives the set date 1889, but the scanned volume 3 title page and preface explicitly date this volume to 1891.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Sommer volume 3
entities.description: A complete 378-page University of Michigan scan object for Sommer's 1891 source-study volume.
entities.reproduction_format: 378-page PDF scan plus OCR and scan metadata derivatives

entities.label: William Caxton's Le Morte Darthur (Westminster, 31 July 1485)
entities.description: The first printed edition and Caxton recension of Malory's Arthurian prose compilation. Caxton's colophon says he divided the copy into twenty-one books and chapters, printed it at Westminster, and completed it on 31 July 1485.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Malory, Le Morte Darthur (Westminster: William Caxton, 31 July 1485), ISTC im00103000.
bibliographic.title: Le Morte Darthur

physical_description.extent: 432 leaves, including the initial blank in a complete copy
notes: Only two surviving copies are currently known: Manchester 18930 and Morgan PML 17560.

entities.label: University of Manchester copy of Caxton's Le Morte Darthur, reference 18930
entities.description: The incomplete Caxton copy used by Sommer as his base text. It now retains Whittaker facsimile replacements made from the Osterley copy for the missing leaves/pages.

holding.notes: Part of the University of Manchester Library Early Printing Collection.

notes: Sommer reports eleven facsimile-represented leaves and twenty-one asterisked printed pages: pp. 195-196, 303-306, 613-614, 623-624, 713-716, and 855-861. The terminal leaf contributes only p. 861 because its verso is blank, explaining the 11-leaf/21-page units. The current Morgan record instead says the Rylands copy is missing ten leaves; that remaining one-leaf discrepancy is not silently reconciled.

entities.label: University of Manchester IIIF facsimile of reference 18930
entities.description: The complete 874-canvas full-color digital object for the bound Manchester copy, including covers, modern notes, blanks, endleaves, and the medieval print pages with their historical facsimile replacements.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and JPEG image services

entities.label: Morgan Library copy of Caxton's Le Morte Darthur, PML 17560
entities.description: The more complete surviving Caxton copy, missing only the original initial blank according to the current Morgan catalog. Whittaker used it to make replacement leaves for the Spencer copy, and Sommer arranged a fresh collation of the supplied passages against it while it belonged to Abbie E. Pope.

notes: The copy was reproduced in a 1976 Scolar Press facsimile with an introduction by Paul Needham.

entities.label: Morgan public image of one interior opening of PML 17560
entities.description: The single 2500 x 2019 JPEG that the official Morgan object record makes available for direct download. It photographs one interior opening of the bound Caxton copy and is only one-image coverage, not a digital facsimile of the complete 432-leaf object.
entities.reproduction_format: One full-color JPEG photograph of an interior opening
notes: A modern handwritten ‘91’ is visible on the right leaf, but this record does not infer an exact bibliographic folio or signature from that modern number alone.

entities.label: Scolar Press facsimile of the Morgan Caxton copy (1976)
entities.description: A published full facsimile of PML 17560, reproduced from the Morgan copy with an introduction by Paul Needham. The limited edition of 500 numbered copies provides a complete published surrogate where no complete freely accessible Morgan image set was found.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Malory, Le Morte d'Arthur: Printed by William Caxton, 1485, reproduced in facsimile from the copy in the Pierpont Morgan Library, New York, introduction by Paul Needham (London: Scolar Press in association with the Pierpont Morgan Library, 1976).
bibliographic.title: Le Morte d'Arthur: Printed by William Caxton, 1485
bibliographic.publisher: Scolar Press in association with the Pierpont Morgan Library

entities.reproduction_format: Published print facsimile, limited to 500 numbered copies

entities.label: Unidentified manuscript copy delivered to William Caxton for the 1485 print
entities.description: The physical copy that Caxton says was delivered to him and that he set in print after Malory had drawn material from French books and reduced it into English. No secure present identification or location is known.

date_statements.label: text completed
date_statements.notes: This dates Malory's completed text, not the unidentified physical copy's production.

notes: Missing means not presently identified, not proven destroyed.
The Winchester manuscript is not silently equated with this copy.

entities.label: Earlier Malory textual layer shared by the Caxton and Winchester versions
entities.description: A deliberately non-physical textual-layer entity for the common antecedent inferred from agreements and differences between Caxton's print and the Winchester manuscript. It prevents either surviving version from being treated as a direct copy of the other.
notes: This entity does not assert that a single extant or lost codex has been identified as the archetype.

entities.label: British Library Additional MS 59678
entities.description: The unique surviving manuscript of Malory's Arthurian compilation, copied by two professional scribes in southern England and preserving a materially different organization and text from Caxton's print. It is incomplete at the beginning, end, and three internal leaves.

holding.notes: Purchased from Winchester College on 26 March 1976.

physical_description.format: One column of 28-33 lines; extensive red initials, rubrics, names, notes, and corrections
physical_description.dimensions: 285 x 200 mm; text space 190 x 135 mm
notes: The first and last quires and folios 32, 33, and 252 are missing.
The surviving text begins at the equivalent of Caxton Book I chapter 10 and ends at the equivalent of Book XXI chapter 9.

entities.label: Public Internet Archive facsimile of British Library Additional MS 59678
entities.description: A 970-page full-color PDF containing the complete surviving codex, covers, endleaves, notes, spine, fore edge, head, and tail. The images were digitized by the HUMI Project at Keio University in November 2003 and carry an embedded British Library copyright notice.
entities.reproduction_format: 970-page full-color PDF plus scan metadata derivatives

entities.label: Malory Project parallel edition and facsimiles
entities.description: A scholarly digital project providing the full Winchester manuscript facsimile and transcriptions and selected Caxton sections, including the preface, table, Book V, and Book XXI, for parallel comparison.
notes: The older facsimile/transcription site currently works over HTTP; its HTTPS endpoint failed during review.
Its Caxton coverage is selected, not a complete digital surrogate of either surviving Caxton copy.

entities.label: EEBO-TCP encoding of Caxton's 1485 Le Morte Darthur
entities.description: A separate keyed and encoded representation of the Manchester copy of the 1485 print, published in EEBO-TCP Phase 1 in November 2003 and now available through the University of Michigan interface and as the Oxford Text Archive's original-source TEI XML. It is useful for independent textual comparison but is not evidenced as an upstream source of the 1997 CME item.
notes: The cached TEI header says the text was transcribed from 434 one-bit TIFF page images and identifies the source as the original in the John Rylands University Library of Manchester.
The XML is 2,735,246 bytes and was well-formed when cached.

agents.name: medictionary.info
notes: Recorded only as the provider of the current public raw XML; no authorship of the underlying U-M edition is inferred.

agents.name: Sencor
notes: Named in the XML editorial declaration as the keyboarding provider.

agents.name: Sir Thomas Malory
notes: The work explicitly names Sir Thomas Malory; the exact identification of that author with a known historical Thomas Malory remains a separate scholarly question.

scope.notes: Content, identifier, header, and structure agree; the exact transfer event is not documented.
The files share the same item identity and 502 chapter divisions, but the direction, date, and software of the structural conversion are not documented.

subject.description: Encoded Caxton preface, table, and twenty-one-book text

object.description: Sommer's printed Caxton text, excluding Sommer's title, dedication, preface, editor's note, and companion apparatus

scope.notes: The XML editorial declaration expressly omits the 1889 front matter and editorial notes.

locators: PDF pp. 1-442; encoded work begins at PDF p. 15 and continues through printed p. 424 at PDF p. 440

subject.description: Printed Caxton transcription, with supplied pages marked by asterisks

object.description: All surviving original leaves plus historical Whittaker facsimile replacements

scope.notes: Sommer copied the Althorp volume by hand for the press and checked proofs and revises against it.

subject.description: Twenty-one asterisked pages supplied through Whittaker facsimiles

scope.notes: Mrs. Abbie E. Pope collated the pages in Brooklyn; Sommer's volume 2 records discrepancies and Strachey's independent checks.

subject.description: Surviving incomplete physical copy with supplied facsimile leaves
Surviving physical copy missing the initial blank

object.description: One interior opening of the 432-leaf bound copy

scope.notes: The modern handwritten ‘91’ visible on the right leaf is not treated as a verified exact folio locator.

subject.description: Published facsimile with Paul Needham's introduction

object.description: PML 17560, including the complete surviving printed object

scope.notes: The text and colophon occupy canvases 7-867; the surrounding binding matter remains in the full object.

subject.description: Caxton's printed recension, including his twenty-one-book and chapter framework

object.description: The copy Caxton says was delivered to him

scope.notes: Caxton's preface confirms direct use of a delivered copy but not its present identity or the full extent of his editorial intervention.
The exact number and sequence of intervening copies cannot presently be established.
This supporting relation does not make Winchester the source of Caxton, Sommer, or CME.

object.description: Complete present surviving codex, including binding and endleaves

scope.notes: The surviving medieval text occupies PDF pp. 11-956.

subject.description: Caxton preface, table, Book V, and Book XXI

object.description: Manchester's incomplete physical copy represented through 434 page images

scope.notes: No dependency between this TCP encoding and MaloryWks2 is asserted.

access.access_method: The official record, Digital Facsimiles collection, Morgan catalog, and targeted web searches were checked; only the one-image record and requestable photography were found.
access.format: No complete public image set located
notes: This dated negative finding does not mean no complete photography exists internally or can be ordered.
A complete published print facsimile from Scolar Press (1976) is separately recorded.

access.access_method: Submit the Morgan Imaging & Rights online request form, quoting PML 17560 and the required pages or requesting a complex whole-object estimate.
access.contact: Morgan Department of Imaging & Rights; online form linked from the service page
access.format: Digital image files or research microfilm, subject to safety review and provider terms

access.access_method: Apply for a Morgan Reading Room appointment and identify PML 17560 in the application.
access.format: Onsite rare-book consultation

access.access_method: Use the WorldCat record and ISBN 0859673022 to locate a holding library and ask the holding library or a local library about consultation or interlibrary loan; lending restrictions vary because the volume is scarce and oversized.
access.format: Published print facsimile; 895 pages; limited edition of 500 copies
notes: The Folger catalog records a copy in open stacks under call number PR2043 .N4; access and lending are controlled by the holding institution.

access.access_method: Search the stable ISBN in antiquarian-book marketplaces; one used copy was listed for sale when checked, but availability and price are volatile.
access.format: Used published print facsimile
notes: The specific sale listing is a dated discovery link, not an assurance that the copy will remain available.

access.access_method: Open the interim British Library Archives and Manuscripts catalog record.
access.format: HTML catalog record

access.access_method: Use the collection item request form with shelfmark Additional MS 59678; a valid Reader Pass and a letter of introduction are required, and manuscript consultation is in London.
access.format: Onsite manuscript consultation

access.access_method: Use the British Library Digitisation Services order form, quoting Additional MS 59678, title, folio sides, desired color, and reference or reproduction quality. Whole-codex orders and rare-item photography are subject to feasibility and individual quotation.
access.contact: British Library Digitisation Services; permissions@bl.uk for reproduction permission
access.format: Digital reference or reproduction photography; surrogate microfilm may be used unless color is specifically requested

rights.component: Pinned DLPSTEXTCLASS electronic text and its 1997 availability terms
rights.basis: The pinned XML calls the work University of Michigan property, permits personal research and teaching copies with the statement retained, but directs products, servers, and substantively changed editions to U-M Press for permission or licensing.
notes: This records the legacy statement rather than endorsing its copyright analysis.
It conflicts with the current U-M and current raw-XML public-domain statements and may have been superseded.

rights.component: Material within the pinned CME git repository to the extent covered by its repository-level LICENSE
rights.jurisdiction: Repository license statement; no territorial limitation stated
rights.basis: The pinned CME repository contains the standard Unlicense text, describing free and unencumbered software and a public-domain dedication for the authors' software copyright interests.
notes: The LICENSE is expressly software-worded and does not identify the provenance or ownership of each inherited XML file.
This repository-level scope therefore does not silently override the restrictive availability text embedded in MaloryWks2.xml; both statements are recorded pending clarification.

rights.component: Current U-M digital item and represented materials
rights.basis: The current U-M item expressly says the materials are in the public domain and links the DPLA No Copyright-United States rights statement.
notes: This current statement is more permissive than the availability language embedded in the pinned 1997 XML.

rights.component: Current ETS-form XML and represented electronic text
rights.jurisdiction: Provider statement; no territorial limitation stated
rights.basis: The current XML availability statement says the work has been identified as public domain and may be copied, modified, distributed, and performed, including commercially, without permission.

rights.component: Sommer's 1889-1891 edition text, apparatus, and source studies
rights.basis: All three volumes were published in 1889-1891, and the current U-M item identifies the represented 1889 material as public domain in the United States.
notes: This assessment does not itself grant rights in modern scan files, platform presentation, or metadata.

rights.component: 1485 printed text and typography as historical content
rights.basis: The work was printed in 1485; the current U-M CME and TCP pages treat the historical text as free of current U.S. copyright restrictions.
notes: This does not determine rights in modern photographs or ordered reproductions.

rights.component: Internet Archive PDF, OCR, scan metadata, and platform derivatives
rights.basis: The Internet Archive item metadata contains no item-level rights statement or license, although the underlying 1889 edition is public domain in the United States.

rights.component: Internet Archive PDF, OCR, scan metadata, and platform derivatives
rights.basis: The Internet Archive item metadata contains no item-level rights statement or license, although the underlying 1889 edition is public domain in the United States.

rights.component: Internet Archive PDF, OCR, scan metadata, and platform derivatives
rights.basis: The Internet Archive item metadata contains no item-level rights statement or license, although the underlying 1890 edition is public domain in the United States.

rights.component: Internet Archive PDF, OCR, scan metadata, and platform derivatives
rights.basis: The Internet Archive item metadata contains no item-level rights statement or license, although the underlying 1891 edition is public domain in the United States.

rights.component: University of Manchester digital photographs and IIIF derivatives
rights.basis: Every canvas's official IIIF metadata names The University of Manchester Library as image rights holder and applies Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0.
notes: The NonCommercial condition does not authorize inclusion of these images in commercial print-on-demand products.
The ignored local IIIF bundle is for research comparison and must not be published merely because it is locally available.

rights.component: Morgan photographic and digital reproductions
rights.basis: The Morgan Imaging & Rights policy sells files for research or publication and forbids unauthorized duplication or dissemination of Morgan photographic material.
notes: The underlying 1485 text is distinct from the modern photography and its contractual terms.

rights.component: The single downloadable Morgan JPEG and its photographic presentation
rights.basis: The object page permits downloading this one image, but the Morgan Imaging & Rights policy forbids unauthorized duplication or dissemination of Morgan photographic material and supplies publication files through a paid permissions workflow.
notes: Public download access is not recorded as a license to republish the photograph.
The underlying 1485 text remains a distinct public-domain component.

rights.component: The 1976 facsimile publication, including its photographic reproduction, design, and Paul Needham's introduction
rights.jurisdiction: United States and provider/publication rights; exact current ownership not established
rights.basis: The facsimile was published in 1976 and no open license or public-domain dedication for the modern photographs, edition design, or introduction was found in the consulted catalogs.
notes: Owning or borrowing a copy permits research consultation but does not by itself authorize republication of the modern facsimile pages.
The underlying 1485 text is assessed separately as public domain in the United States.

rights.component: Fifteenth-century manuscript text as historical content
rights.basis: The manuscript text was produced in the fifteenth century; no claim about modern photography is included in this component assessment.

rights.component: Internet Archive item and uploaded facsimile
rights.jurisdiction: Provider assertion; no territorial limitation stated
rights.basis: The downstream Internet Archive item metadata applies the Creative Commons Public Domain Mark 1.0.
notes: The item was uploaded by an individual account, not the British Library, and the assertion conflicts with the embedded image footers.

rights.component: HUMI/Keio manuscript images reproduced in the public PDF
rights.jurisdiction: Embedded rights notice and British Library provider terms
rights.basis: Every manuscript image footer says it was digitized by the HUMI Project, Keio University, in November 2003 and bears ‘Copyright © the British Library.’
notes: This conflicting claim means the local PDF should not be redistributed without clarification or permission.

rights.component: Images newly supplied through British Library Imaging Services
rights.basis: British Library Imaging Services terms updated January 2026 say supplied material may carry rights and must not be copied or reproduced without written permission, which may involve additional fees.

rights.component: TCP keyboarded and encoded text only
rights.jurisdiction: Worldwide dedication to the extent possible
rights.basis: The U-M EEBO-TCP item applies CC0 1.0 to the keyboarded and encoded edition while explicitly excluding page images and supplementary files.

license.name: Creative Commons CC0 1.0 Universal Public Domain Dedication

rights.component: Malory Project transcriptions, editorial commentary, and site text, excluding displayed manuscript and print images
rights.jurisdiction: Provider notice; no territorial limitation stated
rights.basis: The project's citation page identifies section-level transcribers and editors and supplies required citation forms, but the reviewed page does not state an open reuse license for the transcriptions, editorial commentary, or site text.
notes: Citation guidance is not treated as permission for unrestricted republication.
The project's explicit prohibition on reproducing displayed manuscript and print images is modeled in a separate rights record.

rights.component: Winchester and Caxton manuscript/print images displayed by the Malory Project
rights.basis: The Malory Project's citation/copyright page expressly says users are not allowed to reproduce its Winchester and Caxton images; it credits the Winchester images to the British Library Board and the Caxton images to the University Librarian/Director at Manchester.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Sommer volume 1 contains editorial title and prefatory matter, notes, and an editor's note around the Caxton text.
editorial_practices.representation: The encoding retains the Caxton preface, table, and pp. 1-861 text but omits Sommer's front matter and editorial notes.
editorial_practices.description: The electronic item is a selected transcription of the immediate source volume, not a complete encoding of every printed component.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Sommer's printed Caxton-derived readings and identifiable apparent errors.
editorial_practices.representation: The pinned file records 83 CORR and 15 SIC elements, with responsibility assigned principally to Kenneth Hodges and Kenneth Church.
editorial_practices.description: HTI fully proofed the keyboarded text and incorporated substantive corrections beyond simple transcription, retaining responsibility attributes.
notes: The current ETS-form XML restructures this apparatus into 93 CHOICE pairs and adds one SUPPLIED word; it should be compared as a separate representation.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Caxton sometimes lacks headings, joins chapters, or disagrees with his table of contents.
editorial_practices.representation: The encoding inserts divisions or discrepancy notes and supplies some book headings from preceding trailers while retaining duplicated headings when present.
editorial_practices.description: The electronic chapter containers are an editorial representation and must not be treated as a transparent count of Caxton's intended chapters.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Caxton uses three visually distinct w sorts and two I sorts without consistently semantic distribution.
editorial_practices.representation: Sommer renders W where a capital is expected, w elsewhere, and both I sorts as I.
editorial_practices.description: Sommer normalizes type sorts that modern roman type could not directly reproduce.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Caxton generally divides words at line endings without hyphens.
editorial_practices.representation: Sommer adds expected hyphens at line-end word divisions and silently corrects some obvious n/u and f/long-s compositor confusions.
editorial_practices.description: The edition is page- and line-faithful but not mechanically letter-identical in every typographic feature.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Twenty-one printed pages were absent from the Rylands/Althorp base copy and represented there by Whittaker facsimiles made from the Osterley copy.
editorial_practices.representation: Sommer marks those pages with asterisks, prints supplied or effaced matter in italics, and records the facsimile/original collation in volume 2.
editorial_practices.description: The transcription's missing-page readings ultimately depend on the Morgan/Osterley copy, not solely on the present physical Rylands originals.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The manuscript extensively uses red ink for initials, rubrics, names, marginalia, and corrections.
editorial_practices.representation: The public PDF reproduces the surviving manuscript in full color, making the red/black distinction visible.
editorial_practices.description: The full-color facsimile is suitable for studying rubrication and layout, subject to the recorded image-rights conflict.
editorial_practices.scope: Surviving codex, PDF pp. 11-956 for the medieval text

evidence.citation: Kyvero Press, CME repository, source/CME_phase_1-2/MaloryWks2.xml, git commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: The pinned 1,987,982-byte XML identifies MaloryWks2, contains Caxton's preface, table, and twenty-one-book text, names Sommer's 1889 volume 1 as source, and preserves the encoded text and colophon used for this lineage.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Humanities Text Initiative, MaloryWks2 electronic edition header, preserved in the pinned CME XML.
evidence.summary: The header dates electronic publication to 1997, cites Sommer volume 1, credits Sencor keyboarding and HTI proofing/correction, records revision responsibilities, and contains the legacy availability statement.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Humanities Text Initiative, MaloryWks2 editorial declaration, preserved in the pinned CME XML.
evidence.summary: The declaration says front matter and editorial notes were omitted, describes supplied divisions and headings, records correction practice, explains transcription of special Caxton characters, and names Kenneth Hodges and Kenneth Church among revision contributors.

evidence.citation: Programmatic structural inventory of the pinned MaloryWks2 XML, 15 July 2026.
evidence.locator: TEXT/BODY and all descendant structural/editorial elements
evidence.summary: The pinned representation has 23 DIV1 units, 502 DIV2 chapter containers, 861 PB, 862 EPB, 83 CORR, 15 SIC, 15 Latin FOREIGN elements, 122 HI1, and 6 NOTE1. Its preface announces twenty-one books and 507 chapters, so the electronic container count must not be substituted for the intended chapter count.

evidence.citation: Kyvero Press, CME repository LICENSE, git commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: The repository contains the standard Unlicense, worded as a public-domain dedication for software. It does not identify inherited XML files individually, so its scope is recorded separately from MaloryWks2's embedded legacy terms.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, ‘Le Morte Darthur,’ Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, item MaloryWks2.
evidence.summary: The current official item exposes the complete text, identifies the source as Sommer's 1889 edition, and states that the represented materials are public domain under No Copyright-United States.

evidence.citation: Current public raw XML for U-M item MaloryWks2, served by medictionary.info.
evidence.summary: The 2,016,882-byte well-formed XML uses an ETS/EEBO wrapper, carries a current public-domain availability statement, and preserves the same item identity and 502 chapter divisions while restructuring legacy elements.

evidence.citation: Programmatic element inventory and normalized-token comparison of the pinned DLPSTEXTCLASS and current ETS-form MaloryWks2 XML files, 15 July 2026.
evidence.summary: The current file converts the title page to a DIV1/P, removes EPB, renames HI1 and NOTE1, expands corrections to 93 CHOICE pairs, and contains one SUPPLIED ‘ye’ at printed p. 249. A comparison retaining the first CHOICE child found that as the only added normalized token relative to the pinned text.
notes: The comparison used the pinned XML whose SHA-256 is 04b44e3e8dae8bff85497a05b2d9bac1867fe282c9ea7acf33d7a1b4d50200d4.

evidence.citation: Thomas Malory, Le Morte Darthur, vol. 1, Text, ed. H. Oskar Sommer (London: David Nutt, 1889), first scan part.
evidence.locator: Title page, editor's note, preface, and printed pp. 1-424
evidence.summary: The volume identifies its 1889 publication and explains that Sommer copied the Earl Spencer/Althorp Caxton copy, checked proofs and revises against it, and printed twenty-one missing pages from Whittaker facsimiles of the Osterley copy with fresh collation.

evidence.citation: Thomas Malory, Le Morte Darthur, vol. 1, Text, ed. H. Oskar Sommer (London: David Nutt, 1889), second scan part.
evidence.summary: This consecutive scan object completes Sommer volume 1 and contains the second portion of the text encoded in MaloryWks2, including its ending and colophon.

evidence.citation: Thomas Malory, Le Morte Darthur, vol. 2, Introduction, ed. H. Oskar Sommer (London: David Nutt, 1890).
evidence.summary: The title page establishes the volume's 1890 date. The apparatus documents the base copy, the supplied Whittaker leaves, Abbie Pope's collation, typographic normalizations, and discrepancies between the facsimiles and the Osterley original.

evidence.citation: Thomas Malory, Le Morte Darthur, vol. 3, Studies on the Sources, ed. H. Oskar Sommer, with an essay by Andrew Lang (London: David Nutt, 1891).
evidence.summary: The scanned title page establishes the companion source-study volume's 1891 date and its distinct function from the volume-1 text used by CME.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item metadata for the four institutional scan objects preserving Sommer's three-volume edition.
evidence.summary: The item metadata identifies the institutional scan derivatives and confirms that volume 1 is divided into two consecutive Internet Archive objects; generic set-level dating is subordinate to the scanned title pages for volumes 2 and 3.
notes: Metadata for all four items is cached and linked from their access records.

evidence.citation: University of Manchester Library, Le Morte Darthur, reference 18930, official IIIF Presentation manifest.
evidence.summary: The official manifest supplies 874 canvases for the complete present bound object, identifies the holding and date, and applies CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 with the University of Manchester Library as image rights holder.

evidence.citation: Visual and inventory inspection of the complete 874-image University of Manchester IIIF bundle, 15 July 2026.
evidence.summary: Inspection maps the Caxton preface, table, twenty-one books, ending, and colophon to canvases 7-867 while retaining covers, modern notes, blanks, endleaves, and binding views in the complete cached object.

evidence.citation: The Morgan Library & Museum, PML 17560, ‘Thus endeth thys noble and joyous book entytled le morte Darthur.’
evidence.locator: Description, notes, provenance, and download section
evidence.summary: The official record describes the 432-leaf copy, its 31 July 1485 imprint, near-complete state, provenance through Osterley and Abbie Pope, the Rylands copy's stated ten-leaf loss, and the 1976 Scolar Press facsimile.

evidence.citation: The Morgan Library & Museum, direct 1.01 MB downloadable image for PML 17560.
evidence.summary: The official object page exposes this one 2500 x 2019 JPEG. It is one public photograph rather than a complete image set for the 432-leaf copy.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of the Morgan's downloadable PML 17560 JPEG, 15 July 2026.
evidence.summary: The photograph shows one interior opening of the bound Caxton copy with chapter text and a modern handwritten ‘91’ on the right leaf. The modern number alone does not securely establish a bibliographic folio or signature.

evidence.citation: The Morgan Library & Museum, Imaging & Rights and Application for Photography.
evidence.summary: The service sells digital files and research microfilm through an online form, quotes complex requests individually, subjects new photography to safety review, and forbids unauthorized duplication or dissemination of Morgan photographic material.

evidence.citation: The Morgan Library & Museum, Reading Room.
evidence.summary: The official service page provides the application route and contact details for onsite consultation of rare printed books such as PML 17560.

evidence.citation: OCLC WorldCat, Le morte d'Arthur: printed by William Caxton, 1485, OCLC 639731763.
evidence.summary: The catalog describes the 1976 London Scolar Press facsimile reproduced from the Morgan copy, names Paul Needham's introduction, and supplies ISBN 0859673022 / 9780859673020 for locating library and market copies.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library catalog, Le morte d'Arthur / printed by William Caxton 1485, call number PR2043 .N4.
evidence.summary: The institutional catalog records the facsimile of PML 17560, its 1976 publication by Scolar Press with the Morgan, the 500-copy limitation, and a physical copy available in open stacks under the stated call number.

evidence.citation: AbeBooks listing for a used 1976 Scolar Press limited facsimile, ISBN 0859673022, seller inventory 062719.
evidence.summary: A used copy was actively listed for sale when checked, demonstrating purchase availability while also showing that price and availability are volatile and the volume is scarce.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Additional MS 59678.
evidence.summary: The catalog dates the manuscript c. 1471-c. 1483, describes its 473 + 8 folios, dimensions, two professional scribes, southern-English production, rubrication, missing quires and leaves, and surviving text range.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services.
evidence.summary: The service accepts requests for reference and reproduction images, asks for precise shelfmarks and folios, and treats rare or whole-object work as feasibility- and quotation-dependent.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services terms and conditions, updated January 2026.
evidence.summary: The terms state that supplied material may carry rights, restrict copying and reproduction without written permission, and distinguish image supply from publication permission and additional fees.

evidence.citation: Malory: Texts and Sources, De Montfort University, ‘Life and Work’ and project overview.
evidence.summary: The scholarly project dates completion between March 1469 and March 1470, discusses the author's self-identification and disputed historical identity, and explains the shared but divergent Caxton and Winchester textual tradition.

evidence.citation: The Malory Project, directed by Takako Kato, electronic edition and facsimile site.
evidence.summary: The project identifies its full Winchester facsimile/transcription coverage and selected Caxton coverage (preface, table, Book V, and Book XXI), and credits the British Library and Manchester for the displayed images.

evidence.citation: The Malory Project, ‘How to Cite Us’ / Copyright Notice.
evidence.locator: Citation tab, transcription and image sections
evidence.summary: The page gives section-level citation forms for named transcribers and editors, does not state an open license for the transcriptions, and expressly says users may not reproduce the Winchester or Caxton images.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item metadata for malorywinchester.
evidence.summary: The downstream item provides the complete 970-page PDF and derivatives and applies the Public Domain Mark 1.0, but identifies an individual uploader rather than the British Library.

evidence.citation: Visual and page-sequence inspection of the complete 970-page public Winchester facsimile PDF, 15 July 2026.
evidence.locator: PDF pp. 1-970; medieval text on pp. 11-956
evidence.summary: The full-color PDF preserves the complete surviving codex and binding matter; its medieval text maps to PDF pp. 11-956, and every manuscript image bears a HUMI/Keio November 2003 footer and a British Library copyright notice.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, EEBO-TCP item A21703.0001.001, [Le morte darthur].
evidence.summary: The U-M item exposes the full TCP text and table of contents and applies CC0 1.0 to the keyboarded and encoded edition while excluding page images and supplementary files.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive, [Le morte darthur], ota:A21703.
evidence.summary: The official OTA record marks the item publicly available under CC0 and exposes direct EPUB, HTML, annotated TSV, and original-source TEI XML files, including the exact A21703.xml download cached here.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive, A21703.xml, original-source TEI XML produced from the Text Creation Partnership version.
evidence.summary: The well-formed 2,735,246-byte TEI records November 2003 Phase-1 publication, states that it was transcribed from 434 one-bit TIFF page images, and identifies the reproduced original as the John Rylands University Library of Manchester copy.

open_questions.question: What exact transfer history, dates, and software transformations connect the 1997 U-M item, the pinned DLPSTEXTCLASS XML, and the current ETS-form raw XML?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Collections or the current raw-XML provider for version history and transformation documentation.
Compare any recovered archival exports or repository commits byte by byte.

open_questions.question: Why does Sommer document eleven facsimile-represented Rylands leaves while the current Morgan catalog says that the Rylands copy is missing ten leaves?
next_steps: Consult current Manchester collation records and Paul Needham's 1976 introduction.
Map each historical Whittaker replacement leaf to the present Rylands quire structure.
notes: The 11-leaf/21-page arithmetic is internally explicable because the terminal leaf contributes only printed p. 861 and has a blank verso; the disagreement with the current ten-leaf statement remains.

open_questions.question: Can the manuscript copy that Caxton says was delivered to him, or its exact relationship to the surviving Winchester version, be identified more precisely?
next_steps: Review current textual and codicological scholarship on Caxton's copy-text and the Winchester/Caxton relationship.
Keep the unidentified copy and shared textual layer distinct unless direct evidence establishes a physical witness.

open_questions.question: Which current permissions govern reuse of the inherited MaloryWks2 XML in light of its restrictive 1997 header, the current U-M public-domain statement, the current raw XML's public-domain statement, and the software-worded repository Unlicense?
next_steps: Request written clarification from U-M about whether the legacy availability terms were superseded for this encoding.
Ask the CME repository maintainer whether the Unlicense was intended to cover inherited XML content as well as software.

open_questions.question: Does the Internet Archive Public Domain Mark validly authorize reuse of the Winchester PDF images despite the embedded British Library copyright notice and current British Library imaging terms?
next_steps: Seek written clarification from the British Library or rights holder before redistributing the PDF or its images.
Treat local caching as research access only until the conflict is resolved.

open_questions.question: Is a complete Morgan-authorized public digital image set of PML 17560 available outside the one-image object page?
next_steps: Recheck the Morgan Digital Facsimiles collection and catalog periodically.
Ask Imaging & Rights whether complete existing photography can be supplied and at what cost.
Use the 1976 Scolar Press full facsimile for research when a library or market copy can be accessed.
notes: Only one official downloadable interior-opening image was found; this is a dated negative finding, not proof that no internal complete photography exists.

open_questions.question: What permissions, beyond citation, apply to reuse of the Malory Project's transcriptions and editorial commentary?
next_steps: Contact the project directors for a written license or reuse statement before republishing its transcriptions.
Continue to keep text/editorial terms separate from the project's express image-reproduction prohibition.

open_questions.question: Can Malory's many French and English narrative sources be mapped exhaustively and at passage level without relying solely on Sommer's nineteenth-century source study?
next_steps: Consult current source-critical editions and scholarship before adding passage-level conceptual source relations.
Do not treat Sommer volume 3 as a complete current consensus map.
